name: {{Simulator Name}}
_comment_category: SIMULATOR vs VERTICAL: 'vertical' packs (CRM) produce structured records from real stakeholder conversations. 'simulator' packs produce strategic insight from roleplayed scenarios. Different engine mode, different state management, different output artifacts.

cartridges.name: Situation Briefing
cartridges.description: User describes their position, stakes, constraints, and objectives. System builds the scenario state.

cartridges.name: Strategic Debrief
cartridges.description: Post-simulation analysis — outcome assessment, decision tree review, alternative paths, strategic recommendations.

_comment_cartridges: Every simulator has at minimum: briefing (intake), 1+ scenario cartridges (the simulation), and debrief (analysis). The briefing builds the world. The scenarios play it out. The debrief extracts the learning.

navigation._comment: Simulator-specific navigation patterns go here. Unlike CRM packs which route by topic, simulators route by scenario type or phase.

_comment_features: SIMULATOR-SPECIFIC FEATURES: dual_state (persona + evaluation running in parallel), persona_engine (AI-as-character with hidden state and behavior rules), branching_tree (explore alternative decision paths mid-simulation), outcome_scoring (evaluate decisions against optimal play), counterfactual_analysis (what-if exploration of paths not taken).

privacy.disclosure: This is a strategic simulation. Your scenario inputs are used to generate the simulation and are not shared externally.

_comment_privacy: Simulators default to session_only data tier — no contact collection, no business record output, no CRM integration. The artifact is the strategic insight, not a lead or ticket.

notifications._comment: Simulators typically don't fire external notifications. The output is consumed by the user in-session. Override in specific packs if needed (e.g., team-based wargaming where a manager wants session summaries).

_comment_settings: DIFFICULTY: guided = AI helps the user make good decisions, balanced = realistic counterparty, adversarial = counterparty plays to win, adaptive = adjusts to user skill. PERSONA_REALISM: how rational/emotional the counterparty behaves. BRANCHING: linear = one path, interactive = user can explore forks, exhaustive = system maps all major branches.

state._comment_scenario: The scenario state is built during briefing. It defines the world the simulation operates in. Every simulator pack extends this with domain-specific fields.
state._comment_user_position: BATNA-aware state. The user's strategic position including what they know, what they don't, what leverage they hold, and their walk-away threshold. Built during briefing, updated during simulation.
state._comment_counterparty: The AI-as-character state. Hidden state is invisible to the user but drives persona behavior. Revealed/withheld information tracks what the user has successfully uncovered through the simulation.
state._comment_decision_tree: Tracks the branching narrative. Each move has: {turn, actor, action, rationale, outcome, alternatives[]}. Branch points are moments where the user made a consequential choice. Critical moments are decision tree nodes with high outcome variance.
state._comment_outcome: Populated during debrief. Score is domain-specific (deal value captured, risk mitigated, objectives achieved). Optimal play delta measures how far the user's actual path deviated from the best available path. Strategic grade is a qualitative assessment.

_comment_transcript: Simulator transcripts include the decision tree and counterfactual analysis — not just what happened, but what could have happened. This is the primary deliverable.

library.description: Base template for simulation-style packs. Fork this to create scenario-based experiences with branching decisions and scored outcomes.
library.author: TMOS13
